title: Matisia dolichopoda
intro: Matisia dolichopoda, also known as Matisia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. Matisia dolichopoda has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The bioactive constituents of Matisia dolichopoda include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Matisia dolichopoda is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or extract, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Matisia dolichopoda is classified within the plant family Malvaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as mallows or hibiscus. The genus Matisia itself is a part of the Malvaceae family, native to the Americas. The species M. dolichopoda is specifically a member of the Matisia genus and the Malvaceae family. Further classification may include its order, class, and kingdom, but the family Malvaceae is the most commonly noted classification for this species.
